assertions.description: In Rev A, U2's VCC pin (pin 20) is on __unnamed_35 — a floating net not connected to +3.3V or +5V. Similarly U2 pin 13 (A2) and pin 19 (~OE) are on separate floating unnamed nets. Rev A1 corrects this: U2 VCC moves to +5V and GND to GND. The analyzer faithfully captures this difference: Rev A has 73 total nets while Rev A1 has 70 (the 3 floating unnamed nets are eliminated). The design_observations does not generate an explicit warning about the floating VCC pin, but the net data faithfully represents the schematic state.

On the +5V rail, C7 and C8 have value 'NA(22uF/6.3V/0603)' — the 'NA' prefix followed by the actual spec in parentheses. The capacitance parser cannot extract '22uF' from this non-standard format, so these caps are excluded from the decoupling_analysis. Only C13 (value '100nF', directly parseable) is found, giving a total of 0.1uF with has_bulk=false. The design_observations then incorrectly warns that there is no bulk capacitance on +5V. The same issue affects Rev A1. The parser should attempt to extract capacitance values from parenthetical content in 'NA(...)' formatted values.
